annotations.label: Borel algebra and Borel set

anchor.snippet: algebra generated by all open subsets

annotations.note: `borel α` is defined as the σ-algebra generated by the open sets of a topological space.

annotations.label: Left and right translates

anchor.snippet: then we define the left and right translates

annotations.note: Translation maps `(g * ·)` and `(· * g)` are used pervasively (e.g. `map_mul_left_eq_self`) but a single dedicated `translate` definition is not separately introduced.

anchors.snippet: then we define the left and right translates

annotations.label: Translates map Borel sets to Borel sets

anchor.snippet: Left and right translates map Borel sets onto Borel sets.

annotations.note: In a topological group with `BorelSpace` and `MeasurableMul`, multiplication by a constant is measurable, hence sends Borel sets to Borel sets.

annotations.label: Left-translation-invariant measure

anchor.snippet: is called left-translation-invariant if for all Borel subsets

annotations.note: `IsMulLeftInvariant μ` axiomatizes `map (g * ·) μ = μ`.

annotations.label: Right-translation-invariant measure

anchor.snippet: is called right-translation-invariant if for all Borel subsets

annotations.note: `IsMulRightInvariant μ` axiomatizes `map (· * g) μ = μ`.

annotations.note: Mathlib obtains a right-invariant measure by inverting a left Haar measure via `Measure.inv`; there is no separate `IsRightHaarMeasure` class.

annotations.note: Lebesgue `volume` on ℝ is an additive Haar measure, established in `MeasureTheory.Measure.Lebesgue.EqHaar`.

anchor.snippet: to be the infimum of numbers

annotations.note: Mathlib's construction uses `haarContent` on compacts rather than Cartan's compactly-supported-function infimum, but yields the same Haar measure.

anchor.snippet: Haar measure can be constructed easily as the measure induced

annotations.note: No construction of Haar measure on a Lie group via a left-invariant top form was found.

annotations.label: Existence and uniqueness of right Haar measure

anchor.snippet: there exists a unique (up to multiplication by a positive constant) right-translation-invariant Borel measure

annotations.note: Right Haar measures are obtained as `μ.inv` of a left Haar measure, with `IsMulRightInvariant` instance; uniqueness follows from left-side uniqueness.

annotations.label: Relationship between left and right Haar measures via inversion

anchor.snippet: let us denote by

annotations.note: Inverting a left-invariant measure yields a right-invariant measure (`Measure.inv`).

annotations.label: Left translate of a right Haar measure is right invariant

anchor.snippet: The left translate of a right Haar measure is a right Haar measure.

annotations.note: Mathlib has translation-of-invariant-measure lemmas; this exact `(g·)*ν` is right-invariant statement is implicit in the modular-character development.

annotations.label: Modular function (Haar modulus)

anchor.snippet: called the Haar modulus

annotations.note: `modularCharacterFun g` is defined so that `map (· * g) μ = modularCharacterFun g • μ` for a left Haar measure.

annotations.label: Modular function is a continuous homomorphism

anchor.snippet: The modular function is a continuous group homomorphism

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `modularCharacter : G →* ℝ≥0` (homomorphism property formalized); continuity as a function is not asserted in the file.

annotations.label: Unimodular group

anchor.snippet: A group is called unimodular if the modular function is identically

annotations.note: The term `unimodular` appears only in a docstring comment; no `IsUnimodular` typeclass/predicate exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Non-unimodular affine group of the line

anchor.snippet: An example of a non-unimodular group is the group of affine transformations

annotations.note: The non-unimodularity of the ax+b group is not formalized.

annotations.label: Existence of semi-invariant measures on homogeneous spaces

anchor.snippet: A necessary and sufficient condition for the existence of such a measure

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes Haar quotient measures via fundamental domains; the general modular-character criterion for semi-invariant measures is not stated as such.

annotations.label: GL_2 modulo upper triangular matrices has no semi-invariant measure

anchor.snippet: is the subgroup of upper triangular matrices

annotations.note: The non-existence example on GL₂/B is not formalized.

annotations.label: Haar integral

anchor.snippet: This integral is called the Haar integral

annotations.note: The Haar integral is the Bochner integral against a Haar measure (`integral_mul_left_eq_self` records its key property).

annotations.label: Left invariance of the Haar integral

anchor.snippet: One property of a left Haar measure

annotations.note: `∫ x, f (g * x) ∂μ = ∫ x, f x ∂μ` for left-invariant `μ`.

annotations.label: Non-existence of countably additive invariant measure on all subsets

anchor.snippet: it is impossible to define a countably additive left-invariant regular measure on all subsets

annotations.note: No Vitali-type impossibility theorem for translation-invariant measures on all subsets was located.

annotations.label: Sufficiency of a left-invariant Radon measure

anchor.snippet: it suffices to exhibit a left-invariant Radon measure

annotations.note: Mathlib gives sufficient conditions (left-invariant, regular, positive-finite on a compact set with interior) for a measure to be Haar.

annotations.label: Weil's converse theorem

anchor.snippet: if a group has a left invariant measure with a certain separating property

annotations.note: Weil's converse theorem reconstructing a topological group from an invariant measure is not formalized.
